messages.content:
Hi, please help me to paraphrase and summarise this for my report: The school provides structures and processes for PD in identified key areas of focus.
a. Time and space were provided for all PD activities. With the implementation of HBL Day, all staff meetings and PD sessions were conducted on HBL Day. This helped streamline staff activities, which used to be held in the afternoons on weekdays. An email would be sent a week before HBL Day so that staff knew their involvement and the respective timing and venue to report.[
b. A school-wide level training on design and use of rubrics for assessment was conducted in 2022, with heightened importance of performance task to engage our learners and less reliance on pen-and-paper assessment. Two mass workshops were conducted to introduce the rubrics principles, with a 30 minute consultation session in-between workshops for each department/unit. To ensure transference of learning, all IP department planned for at least a WA on performance task for any subject, level or stream each year.
c. In alignment with the changing educational landscape and MOE initiatives, together with data from LNA, the school focused on three areas of practice under SFEd – ePed, DI and CCE. To ensure that staff has a baseline competency in these areas, coaching sessions were planned for new/beginning teachers, including current teachers who require support in these areas. These teachers would be identified based on the level of practice indicated on SFEd, and also through nominations by HODs. The coaching sessions were helmed by TLs, who were deployed based on their expertise in the respective areas. Following feedback in 2022, the coaching sessions were increased to 6 sessions to better engage the teachers and provide a longer runway for implementation and feedback.
d. During formal lesson observations, SLs identified teachers who were skillful in delivering e-Ped, DI lessons (with possibility of values-infused). These teachers were then approached to conduct lesson demonstrations in June or Nov/Dec, so that other teachers could benefit from their sharings. Participants were required to provide feedback on the lesson observed to the teacher, and suggest how they would like to apply what they learnt from the lessons. The feedback received will also be given to the teachers so that they could seek improvement and growth for their lessons. [
e. Teachers were identified by HODs to open their classroom for lesson observations. All teachers were encouraged to sign up for the Open Classroom to observe how authentic teaching and learning could be conducted, specific to e-Ped, DI and values-infused lessons. All teachers were also to observe a peer’s lesson, and be observed for one lesson, with a 3-2-1 reflection to be completed after the peer lesson observation. This provides an open culture of learning where teachers could do so at their own pace and time.

The school has implemented structures and processes for professional development (PD) in specific areas of focus. To streamline staff activities, all PD sessions and staff meetings now take place on HBL Day instead of in the afternoons on weekdays. Staff are notified of their involvement in advance through an email. In 2022, a school-wide training on the design and use of rubrics for assessment was conducted. Two mass workshops were held, along with consultation sessions for each department/unit. To ensure the transfer of learning, the school planned for performance tasks in all IP departments. In alignment with MOE initiatives, the school focused on ePed, DI, and CCE and provided coaching sessions for new/beginning teachers and current teachers in need of support. Teachers with expertise in the respective areas were chosen as coaches. Lessons delivered by skillful teachers were observed, and participants provided feedback to the observed teacher. Teachers were encouraged to open their classrooms for lesson observations and to observe their peers. A culture of open learning was fostered, allowing teachers to learn at their own pace and time.

Hi can you help me to paraphrase and summarise this for my report:	In alignment with the changing educational landscape and MOE initiatives, together with data from LNA, the school focused on three areas of practice under SFEd – ePed, DI and CCE. To ensure that staff has a baseline competency in these areas, coaching sessions were planned for new/beginning teachers, including current teachers who require support in these areas. These teachers would be identified based on the level of practice indicated on SFEd, and also through nominations by HODs. The coaching sessions were helmed by TLs, who were deployed based on their expertise in the respective areas. Following feedback in 2022, the coaching sessions were increased to 6 sessions to better engage the teachers and provide a longer runway for implementation and feedback.

To adapt to the changing educational landscape and MOE initiatives, the school focused on three areas of practice - ePed, DI, and CCE. In order to ensure that staff had a basic competency in these areas, coaching sessions were organized for new and beginning teachers, as well as for current teachers who needed support. These teachers were identified based on their level of practice indicated on SFEd, as well as through nominations by HODs. The coaching sessions were led by TLs who had expertise in the respective areas. Based on feedback received in 2022, the number of coaching sessions was increased to six in order to better engage teachers and provide a longer period for implementation and feedback.

Hi can you help me to paraphrase and summarise this for my report:	During formal lesson observations, SLs identified teachers who were skillful in delivering e-Ped, DI lessons (with possibility of values-infused). These teachers were then approached to conduct lesson demonstrations in June or Nov/Dec, so that other teachers could benefit from their sharings. Participants were required to provide feedback on the lesson observed to the teacher, and suggest how they would like to apply what they learnt from the lessons. The feedback received will also be given to the teachers so that they could seek improvement and growth for their lessons.

Skilled teachers in delivering e-Ped, DI lessons (with the possibility of values-infused) were identified by SLs during formal lesson observations. These teachers were then invited to conduct lesson demonstrations in June or November/December, allowing other teachers to benefit from their expertise. Participants of these demonstrations were required to provide feedback to the observed teacher, suggesting how they could apply what they learned from the lesson. The feedback received will also be shared with the teachers, enabling them to seek improvement and growth in their own lessons.

Hi can you help me to paraphrase and summarise this for my report: 	Teachers were identified by HODs to open their classroom for lesson observations. All teachers were encouraged to sign up for the Open Classroom to observe how authentic teaching and learning could be conducted, specific to e-Ped, DI and values-infused lessons. All teachers were also to observe a peer’s lesson, and be observed for one lesson, with a 3-2-1 reflection to be completed after the peer lesson observation. This provides an open culture of learning where teachers could do so at their own pace and time.

HODs identified teachers to open their classrooms for lesson observations, while all teachers were encouraged to participate in the Open Classroom program. This program allowed teachers to observe authentic teaching and learning methods, specifically focused on e-Ped, DI, and values-infused lessons. Additionally, all teachers were required to observe a lesson taught by a peer and have one of their own lessons observed. After the peer lesson observation, a 3-2-1 reflection was completed. This approach fostered an open culture of learning, allowing teachers to engage at their own pace and convenience.

Hi, could you help me to paraphase for my report: SLs and ROs monitor the application of skills and knowledge at three levels: individual, departmental and school. 
a.	At the individual level, ROs leverage the work review sessions to communicate the staff's strengths and areas for growth based on lesson observation, book/file checks and contributions in CCAs. As for the areas for improvement, learning and development activities are discussed to help the staff upgrade their performance. Officers with higher potential are assigned responsibilities at a higher level to develop their competencies. As reflective practitioners, staff recorded their TnL practices in their Reflection Journal. Prior to 2023, all Reflection Journals were submitted to RO every even Monday for their comments and endorsements. Upon feedback from staff and HODs, this process was made less onerous in the year 2023. Instead of submitting a reflection journal, all staff now key into their respective department Padlet the pedagogies, strategies, or even new ICT tools they have used in class. This approach was well-received by staff as they would now know what their peers had tried and could better share their teaching ideas amongst one another.

At the individual level, ROs utilize work review sessions to provide feedback to staff based on observations of their lessons, checks of their materials, and contributions in CCAs. Strengths and areas for growth are communicated, and learning and development activities are discussed to help improve performance. Staff members with higher potential are assigned higher-level responsibilities to develop their skills. As reflective practitioners, staff record their teaching and learning practices in their Reflection Journal. Previously, Reflection Journals were submitted to ROs every even Monday for comments and endorsements. However, in 2023, this process was made less burdensome. Instead of submitting a journal, staff now enter their pedagogies, strategies, or new ICT tools used in class into their department Padlet. This new approach has been well-received as it allows staff to learn from each other's experiences and share teaching ideas more effectively.

Hi, could you help me to paraphase for my report: 	At the departmental level, the transfer of the learning can be seen in terms of the application in the PLT projects, continued implementation of using rubrics for assessment, department sharings on key courses, including those reflected on department padlet, etc. Some teachers who were at risk were also assigned to attend relevant courses and share at the department meetings to ensure transference of learning.

At the departmental level, the application of learning can be observed through PLT projects, the ongoing use of rubrics for assessment, and departmental sharing of key courses. This includes sharing resources and ideas on the department padlet. Teachers who were identified as being at risk were assigned to attend specific courses and were asked to share their learnings at department meetings, ensuring that the knowledge gained is transferred effectively.

Hi, could you help me to paraphase for my report: 	At the school level, SLs and ROs monitor adherence to learning and development plans as well as alignment of learning activities to school goals and priorities through the student’s survey, staff survey, staff LNA, formal lesson observations, sitting in at department meeting and PLT sessions, etc.

At the school level, SLs and ROs oversee the implementation of learning and development plans and ensure that learning activities are aligned with the school's goals and priorities. This is done through various means such as student surveys, staff surveys, staff LNA (Learning Needs Analysis), formal lesson observations, attending department meetings, and participating in PLT sessions. These monitoring activities help to gauge the effectiveness of the plans and ensure that they are in line with the overall objectives of the school.

Hi can you help me to paraphase and retain the number of paragraphs in this report as it is: The school monitors and reviews staff L&D, and devises strategies for improvement.
a.	The application of learning is monitored through the SOWs, lesson observations, book/file checks, open classroom, and peer observation. For open classroom and peer observations, 3-2-1 reflection is used to promote positive learning culture among teachers. 
b.	The SSD reviews staff development programmes from staff surveys and  conversations with IP HODs. For example, in the 2022 review, revision of the DI coaching programme was made where the concept of coach and coachee were removed and replaced with skill-based coaching, with the aim of achieving a baseline standard for DI for all new teachers who joined the school and beginning teachers whowere new to service.  
c.	The KPs monitor the application of learning in department-based PLTs. Feedback gathered from staff surveys is used to inform professional development decisions. In 2023, school-based PLTs are formed based on a ground-up approach from teachers. In total, three teams of school-based PLT tackle school-wide issues such as motivating low progress learners in the classroom. PLT provides a platform to drive innovation in the school, where projects will be subsequently showcased at the Staff Learning Festival. 
d.	To ensure that the delivery of the CCE packages is learner-centred, time is set aside for Class Mentors (CMs) to meet once a fortnight to discuss and enhance the strategies for the lesson that will be conducted in the following week. The CCE Com has also complemented the CCE packages by converting them to PearDeck lessons to focus more on student-centred lessons. 
e.	To level up staff competencies in facilitating engaging CCE lessons, in 2022, the school worked with CCE Branch to co-construct and co-facilitate a training session entitled Exploring the HeART of CCE with all staff on facilitation skills.

The school actively monitors and evaluates staff learning and development (L&D) efforts and develops strategies for improvement. This process is divided into multiple sections:
a. The application of learning is closely monitored through various means such as SOWs, lesson observations, book/file checks, open classroom sessions, and peer observations. The use of a 3-2-1 reflection framework encourages a positive learning culture among teachers.
b. The SSD (Staff Support and Development) team reviews staff development programs based on feedback from staff surveys and discussions with IP HODs. For example, in the 2022 review, the DI coaching program was revised, removing the coach and coachee concept and emphasizing skill-based coaching. The aim was to establish a baseline standard for DI for all new and beginning teachers.
c. The KPs (Key Personnel) monitor the application of learning in department-based PLTs (Professional Learning Teams). Feedback gathered from staff surveys helps inform decisions on professional development. In 2023, school-based PLTs are formed through a ground-up approach driven by teachers. Three teams tackle school-wide issues, such as motivating low-progress learners. This platform encourages innovation and projects are showcased at the Staff Learning Festival.
d. To ensure learner-centered delivery of the CCE (Character and Citizenship Education) packages, Class Mentors have dedicated time every fortnight to discuss and enhance lesson strategies for the following week. The CCE Committee has also enhanced the packages by converting them into PearDeck lessons, focusing more on student-centered approaches.
e. In 2022, the school collaborated with the CCE Branch to co-construct and co-facilitate a training session called "Exploring the HeART of CCE" to improve staff competencies in facilitating engaging CCE lessons.

Hi can you help me to paraphrase and summarise this for my report: Staff could take a greater ownership for their own learning by being proactive in attending relevant courses and workshops. At each work review session, staff could lay out specifically their training plans and development, and regularly update their ROs of their progress and development. These included targets for peer observations and open classroom as well.

Staff members have the opportunity to take more ownership of their own learning by actively participating in relevant courses and workshops. During work review sessions, they can outline their training plans and development goals, regularly updating their ROs on their progress. This includes setting targets for peer observations and participating in open classroom sessions.
